title: MOSFET Constant-Current Load Stage
design_question: How to draw a precise, programmable constant current from a device under test (battery, power supply, or LED) while dissipating the power in a MOSFET?
selected_approach: IRFP250N N-MOSFET (200 V, 30 A) in linear mode controlled by LM358 op-amp feedback loop. 0.1 Ω / 5 W sense resistor in source circuit; op-amp inverting input = V_sense, non-inverting = setpoint from MCP4725 12-bit I²C DAC. MOSFET and sense resistor mounted on 200 mm × 150 mm aluminium heatsink. Operating range: 0–5 A, 0–30 V DUT.
rationale: Op-amp feedback linearises MOSFET gate control, providing I_load = V_set / R_sense with < 2 % accuracy limited by op-amp offset. 0.1 Ω sense resistor produces 0–500 mV at full scale, within LM358 common-mode range. MCP4725 DAC allows digital setpoint from Arduino over I²C. Maximum power dissipation 30 A × 0.1 Ω = 3 W in sense resistor; MOSFET dissipates remainder.

alternatives.reason_rejected: Fixed load only; not programmable; large resistor array needed for different test currents.
Commercial units cost £100–400; educational value in building own is significant for understanding feedback control.

verification_notes: FACT: 'MOSFET Linear-Mode Constant-Current Electronic Load' confirms op-amp feedback topology, IRFP250N suitability, and I = V_set / R_shunt formula.
supporting_fact_titles: N-Channel MOSFET as a Low-Side Power Switch
